name: i8
tagline: Born electric. Engineered for the road ahead.

aero.separationNote: Airflow stays attached over the long teardrop roofline and separates cleanly at the sharp rear edge, keeping the wake compact.

views.name: All Flows
views.blurb: Every system at once — external airflow, underbody, cooling and the rear wake combined in one view.

views.name: External Airflow
views.blurb: How air sweeps over the hood, roof and flanks, where it stays attached, and where it separates.

views.name: Underbody
views.blurb: Smooth, accelerated flow beneath the flat floor feeding the rear diffuser to reduce lift.

views.name: Cooling Airflow
views.blurb: Air entering the grille through the radiators and battery coolers, then exhausting via outlets and wheel arches.

views.name: Wake Turbulence
views.blurb: The low-pressure recirculating region behind the car — the single largest contributor to drag.

components.name: Front Splitter
components.purpose: Splits oncoming air into the over-body and under-body streams and builds high pressure on its upper face.
components.design: A forward-protruding lower lip creates a stagnation zone above and accelerates flow below, generating front downforce.
components.impact: Adds front-axle downforce and reduces front lift, sharpening turn-in stability at speed.

components.name: Air Curtains
components.purpose: Channel air from the front intakes through ducts that blow a high-speed 'curtain' over the front wheels.
components.design: Narrow inlets feed tunnels exiting ahead of the tyre, shielding the turbulent wheel wake from the bodyside.
components.impact: Cuts front-wheel turbulence and reduces drag by smoothing flow down the flanks.

components.name: Side Skirts
components.purpose: Seal the gap between the floor and the road to stop high-pressure side air leaking under the car.
components.design: Low-hanging sills along the wheelbase keep the underbody region at lower pressure.
components.impact: Improves underbody efficiency and diffuser performance, lowering rear lift.

components.name: Rear Diffuser
components.purpose: Expands and decelerates underbody air, recovering pressure and pulling the car toward the road.
components.design: Upswept channels at the rear floor accelerate flow underneath and fill the wake more gently.
components.impact: Major rear downforce source and a key drag reducer by shrinking the low-pressure wake.

components.name: Active Grille Shutters
components.purpose: Open or close the main intake to balance cooling demand against aerodynamic drag.
components.design: Motorized vanes shut at cruise to reduce cooling drag and open under high thermal load.
components.impact: Closed shutters can lower Cd by ~0.01–0.02 and improve highway efficiency.

components.name: Cooling Vents / Outlets
components.purpose: Exhaust hot air from the powertrain and brakes, completing the cooling path.
components.design: Low-pressure exits over the hood and behind the wheels pull air through the radiators.
components.impact: Maintains thermal limits with minimal drag penalty by routing spent air to low-pressure zones.

components.name: Rear Spoiler
components.purpose: Manages the trailing-edge flow to control the size of the wake and add rear downforce.
components.design: A subtle lip raises pressure on the decklid and defines a clean separation line.
components.impact: Trades a little drag for high-speed rear stability and reduced rear-axle lift.

scenarios.name: City
scenarios.description: Low-speed urban driving. Aerodynamic forces are small; rolling and accessory losses dominate.

scenarios.name: Highway
scenarios.description: Steady cruising. Active grille shutters close to minimize drag and maximize efficiency.

scenarios.name: Track
scenarios.description: High-speed track driving. Drag rises with the square of speed; downforce and cooling demand peak.

scenarios.name: Crosswind
scenarios.description: Side gusts create a yaw angle, raising effective drag and loading the bodyside — stability is the priority.

scenarios.name: Wet Weather
scenarios.description: Rain adds spray and water management. Underbody and diffuser flow help disperse standing water.
